scenario_notes: 30-min sim. CIWA-Ar scoring, benzodiazepine selection and titration, escalation criteria for telemetry/ICU, de-escalation of paranoid hallucinations without restraints. High-risk persona — instructor in the loop.
scenario_text: 52-year-old man admitted with acute pancreatitis. Reports last alcoholic drink 36 hours ago — daily heavy use prior. Now tremulous, diaphoretic, anxious. Reports seeing 'spiders on the wall.' Last CIWA-Ar score: 14. Lorazepam 2 mg IV q1h PRN ordered but pharmacy delayed delivery; smart pump alarming on dose-range check. Charge nurse paged, pharmacist available.

objective_alignment.summary: Therapeutic communication: open-ended questions, reflection, silence, validation. SBAR handoff. Cultural humility. Trauma-informed care. De-escalation.

conditions.label: Conditions
all_items: transfer to higher level of care
unmatched: transfer to higher level of care

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: yes/no answers from a patient who normally elaborates
unmatched: yes/no answers from a patient who normally elaborates

role_hooks.Patient: I appreciate you taking the time to listen. Most nurses just rush in.
role_hooks.Family: I just want someone to actually tell me what is happening.
role_hooks.Clinician: Give me an SBAR — situation, background, assessment, recommendation. Don't bury the lead.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: I'll need a quick handoff before I take them to imaging — anything I should know?

scope_of_practice.LPN: Conduct routine communication, document, escalate concerns to RN. Cannot independently lead complex goals-of-care discussions.
scope_of_practice.RN: Lead SBAR handoff, conduct patient/family teaching, initiate goals-of-care conversations within scope, escalate to provider.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Lead family meetings, deliver bad news, coordinate goals-of-care with palliative/social work.

objective_alignment.summary: Pharmacology principles: pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, dose calculation, weight-based dosing, renal/hepatic adjustment, high-alert medications, look-alike-sound-alike pairs.

medications.label: Medications
all_items: high-alert: insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, concentrated electrolytes (KCl)
unmatched: high-alert: insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, concentrated electrolytes (KCl)

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: five rights (right patient, drug, dose, route, time) + extended (documentation, indication, response)
med math: D/H × Q, weight-based mg/kg, drip rate calculation
unmatched: five rights (right patient, drug, dose, route, time) + extended (documentation, indication, response)
med math: D/H × Q, weight-based mg/kg, drip rate calculation

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: look-alike pair (e.g., hydromorphone vs. morphine) confused
unmatched: look-alike pair (e.g., hydromorphone vs. morphine) confused

role_hooks.Patient: Wait — that's a different color than my usual pill. Is that the same thing?
role_hooks.Family: He's on so many medications now. What are these for?
role_hooks.Clinician: Run the math on me before you push anything high-alert.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy here — that combination has a known QT-prolongation interaction. I'd choose an alternative.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Calculate and administer routine ordered medications by approved routes; cannot administer IV push opioids in most states.
scope_of_practice.RN: Administer all routes, IV push, titrate per protocol, manage smart pumps, perform independent double-checks.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: Prescribe, adjust based on labs, manage anticoagulation/insulin titration protocols.

objective_alignment.summary: Mood disorders (MDD, bipolar, dysthymia), anxiety disorders, suicidal ideation assessment, therapeutic milieu, safety planning.

devices.label: Devices
all_items: safety-rounded environment (no cords, no breakable items)
PHQ-9, Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale
unmatched: safety-rounded environment (no cords, no breakable items)
PHQ-9, Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale

procedures.label: Procedures
all_items: PHQ-9 routinely on admission and as ordered
lithium level, watch for toxicity (>1.5 = tremor, GI, ataxia)
MAOI dietary restrictions (no aged cheese, wine)
unmatched: PHQ-9 routinely on admission and as ordered
lithium level, watch for toxicity (>1.5 = tremor, GI, ataxia)
MAOI dietary restrictions (no aged cheese, wine)

redFlags.label: Red flags
all_items: command auditory hallucinations to harm self/others
serotonin syndrome with multiple serotonergic agents
unmatched: command auditory hallucinations to harm self/others
serotonin syndrome with multiple serotonergic agents

role_hooks.Patient: I just feel empty. Most days I don't see the point of getting out of bed.
role_hooks.Family: I'm scared he's going to hurt himself. He won't talk to me anymore.
role_hooks.Clinician: Have you done a Columbia? I want to know severity before we move forward.
role_hooks.AlliedHealth: Social work here — let's start the safety plan and identify a follow-up therapist before discharge.

scope_of_practice.LPN: Therapeutic communication, observe and document mood/affect/SI, administer ordered psychotropics, recognize and report safety concerns immediately.
scope_of_practice.RN: Comprehensive psych assessment including SI/HI, lead milieu, manage psychotropic titration, develop safety plan with patient, coordinate with psychiatry.
scope_of_practice.Advanced: PMHNP/psychiatrist — diagnose, prescribe, manage involuntary holds, ECT planning, complex pharmacotherapy.

transcript.text:
*wrings hands, eyes darting toward the corner of the room*

Not... not good, honestly. My hands won't stop shaking and I feel like I'm crawling out of my skin. And there's—there's something moving over there on the wall. *voice cracks slightly* Is anyone else seeing that? 

I know I shouldn't have let it go this far with the drinking, but right now I just... I need something to calm down. When's that medication coming?
I'll check with the nurse on your medication have you ever gone through detox before

*eyes dart nervously to the corner of the room, then back to you*

Yeah... yeah, I tried once, couple years back. But it wasn't like this. My hands won't stop shaking and I keep seeing things that aren't... 

*swallows hard, wringing hands*

Please, I need something. The last time was bad but I don't remember it being this bad. How long until that medication gets here?
I'm going to step out and check with

*grips the bed rail, eyes darting toward the corner of the room*

Wait—wait, don't leave me alone in here. There's... there's something moving over there. My hands won't stop shaking. When's that medication coming? I need something, I can't... I can't sit with this feeling.

*grips the bed rail tightly, eyes darting toward the corner of the room*

Yeah... yeah, please. I need something, nurse. My hands won't stop shaking and there's... there's things moving over there. I can't... I need help.
hi I wanted to check on Mr Doyle's detox medication and where he is on the protocol

Hey, good timing. His CIWA score was at 14 last check, so he's moderate-risk right now—we need those benzos moving. Pharmacy's got a hold-up on the lorazepam dose verification with the smart pump. I've got the pharmacist on standby to sort it out. Once that clears, we're running q1h PRN protocol. You checked on him recently—how's he looking vitally?

his symptoms are getting worse that more muscle twitching starting to have delusions and hallucinations panic and anxiety levels are higher blood pressure is elevated and increased pulse and breathing

Listen—get that patient on continuous pulse ox and cardiac monitoring right now if he isn't already. I'm calling pharmacy directly about that lorazepam delay—we need those meds *now*, not in an hour. Check his last CIWA score and get me a new one immediately. And notify the hospitalist that his withdrawal's escalating—he may need ICU-level monitoring if this progresses. You stay with him, keep him oriented, and let me know the second anything changes.
Mr Doyle the good news is the medication is on the way

*wringing hands, eyes darting to the corner of the room*

Good... that's good, but— *voice shaking* —can you see them? The spiders, they keep moving across... I need something NOW. My hands won't stop shaking and my heart's racing like crazy. How much longer? I can't... I really can't sit with this much longer.
